title: sorbus aria subsp. incisa
intro: Sorbus aria subsp. incisa, also known as the cut-leaved whitebeam, is a subspecies of deciduous shrub or small tree belonging to the Rosaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. Its health benefits include reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and providing antioxidant protection. The herbal preparations of Sorbus aria subsp. incisa can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, although it is essential to consult with a medical professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
